reasoning: The current WTI price (as of Mar 14) is $67.43. For the shortest horizon (Mar 23), the statistical trend tool estimates a 47% chance of the price being above this baseline, balancing a slight recent downward drift against upward momentum over the last week. For the middle horizons (Apr to Sept 2025), I adjust slightly upward (51-54%), primarily because the EIA's latest March STEO characterizes global oil markets as 'tight' until mid-2025, forecasting Brent to reach $75/bbl by Q3 2025 (translating to a WTI price around $70-71, comfortably above the $67.43 baseline). For the longest horizon (Mar 2026), expected global oil production growth (particularly the unwinding of OPEC+ cuts and growing non-OPEC supply) is projected by the EIA and J.P. Morgan to outpace demand, causing prices to fall back into the mid-to-low $60s. Consequently, my probability for 2026 dips slightly below 50%. Overall, probabilities are tightly clustered around 0.50, reflecting the inherent unpredictability (random walk) of commodity markets over extended timelines and conforming to the requirement that uncertainty increases (approaching 0.50) at distant horizons.
